main_question: Which single DCS window best supports calcium/bromine recommendation?
best_result: 50 min best by composite score in the local 45/50/55/60/65 scan; 60 min slightly best on calcium MAE alone
key_conclusion: Use 50 min only as a V3 starting prior; rerun window search after rebuilding candidate features and point sets

main_question: Does direct multi-scale feature concatenation beat a single window?
best_result: 15+30, 15+45, 15+60, 20+60 did not outperform the better single-window baselines
key_conclusion: Do not assume multi-scale concatenation is automatically helpful

main_question: Does a single global PH lag explain T90 well?
best_result: No stable global optimum inside the expected 180-270 min band
key_conclusion: PH should not be treated as a universal fixed-lag main signal

main_question: Can 50 min DCS define reusable stages for conditional PH use?
best_result: 3 stages was the best practical choice; PH(120 min) helped some stages
key_conclusion: Stage structure is only a medium-confidence prior because it depended on legacy feature screening

main_question: Does stage-aware optional PH improve recommendation quality?
best_result: Composite score improved by about +0.0127 over the pure 50 min DCS baseline
key_conclusion: Stage-aware optional PH is promising for recommendation, not mandatory for V3

main_question: Can the current framework produce an over-spec risk signal and how accurate are recommendations?
best_result: Over-spec risk ROC AUC about 0.749; recommendation accuracy degrades on out-of-spec samples
key_conclusion: Useful reference, but do not treat this as a final industrial alert solution

main_question: Which same-sample alert method works best?
best_result: single_logistic_oos had the best ROC AUC and average precision; extra trees only slightly edged F1
key_conclusion: Prefer logistic as the probability source if V3 still explores same-sample alerting

main_question: Which thresholds are plausible for low-miss or low-false-alarm modes?
best_result: Different thresholds trade recall against false alarms sharply
key_conclusion: Threshold policy must be tied to plant use mode, not chosen by F1 alone

main_question: Can temporal persistence logic make the alert industrially cleaner?
best_result: Temporal policies reduced switching and chatter, but did not fundamentally solve false alarms
key_conclusion: Use persistence as signal shaping only; do not treat it as the core breakthrough

main_question: Do multiple small models plus voting outperform the best single model?
best_result: No clear win over single logistic on ranking quality or practical usability
key_conclusion: Do not spend V3 effort on committee methods unless the task definition changes
